positiveClaims: Review menu exposes Import Review Packet and Clear Review Session product entries.
cmd.project.review.importLocalPacket is bridge-allowlisted and registered in the renderer command kernel.
Local JSON review packet intake is main-owned: dialog selection, extension gate, size gate, read, and JSON parse all run in main.
Local JSON review packet intake restats the selected file before read and rejects oversized replacement files before fs.readFile.
Local JSON review packet intake rejects imported write-evidence fields such as receipt, exactTextApplyReceipts, and exactTextAppliedChangeIds before session activation.
Renderer sends intent-only requestId for local packet import and never sends filePath, raw JSON, projectRoot, scenePath, applyOps, or receipt authority.
Successful local packet intake delegates to the existing review packet import session path and opens Review/Comments through canonical runtime command handling.
Clear Review Session clears active review session state and opens Review/Comments with an empty surface.
Capability binding allows local packet import and clear session only on node; web and mobile wrapper remain disabled.
nonClaims: No full review import automation is claimed.
No DOCX review import is claimed by this contour.
No manuscript write is performed by local packet intake.
No cross-scene atomicity or multi-file transaction truth is claimed.
No release readiness, packaged readiness, cross-platform readiness, full npm green, or Y9 admission is claimed.
layerDecisions: importLocalPacket is separate from importPacket so local file intake semantics do not blur with in-memory packet import.
File authority remains in main; renderer command payload remains intent-only.
Review surface state is UI/session preview truth only; project truth remains scene files and manifest binding.
Exact apply and batch exact apply remain behind the existing main-owned safe writer guards.

knownDiagnostic: Exact-apply changed-file allowlist tests were executed while this contour was still dirty and failed only on dirty-diff scope checks; their functional subtests passed. Rerun after delivery clean tree is required for final delivery report.

nextStep: Complete delivery chain, rerun dirty-diff-sensitive checks on clean tree, then keep the next product contour explicit and owner-selected.
